patch.value: Observed in audit #15 on pm-rjqr: codex released its claim after PR #466 merged, the item silently stayed in_progress with claimed_by=none, and it surfaced as phantom WIP until a manual audit reset it. pm release only clears the claim; the status transition is a separate manual step that agents forget at end-of-session. Complements pm-w8q4 (after-the-fact detection) by preventing the drift at the moment it happens. pause-task already bundles release+open, but per ADR pm-pbyu it is sugar slated to fold into claim/release flags (pm-eq4x) — this issue is the release-side flag/hint itself.
pm release leaves the item in_progress with no owner: offer --status open (or hint) so released work does not linger as phantom WIP

pm release gains an ergonomic path to also reset status (flag such as --reopen/--status open, or at minimum a one-line hint that the item remains in_progress and unclaimed); pause-task alias behavior is referenced as the existing combined spelling; MCP pm_release mirrors the option; docs + linked tests; default behavior unchanged

author: codex-agent

patch.value: pm release can leave an item in_progress with no owner, creating phantom work in context views; add a safe status transition or recovery hint so released-but-unowned work returns to a clear state.

message: Backfill description during all-status ecosystem audit

Complements pm-w8q4 (after-the-fact detection) by preventing the drift at the moment it happens. pause-task already bundles release+open, but per ADR pm-pbyu it is sugar slated to fold into claim/release flags (pm-eq4x) — this issue is the release-side flag/hint itself.
